feedback: Strong post. The Rayni personal moment ('I've been manually translating Figma decisions into component specs for months') earns the take. Sentence variation is natural — 'Technically accurate.' and 'Not a coincidence.' land well against the longer expository paragraphs. The ending question is specific and earned, not an engagement farm.

dimension_notes.voice: pass — multiple fragments, 5+ first-person refs, one hedge, natural burstiness

dimension_notes.platform: pass — ~1360 chars in sweet spot, hook works in first 210 chars, genuine specific question
dimension_notes.epistemic: pass — surprised register matches brief, cautious on Stitch production quality
dimension_notes.structure: pass — asymmetric paragraphs, strong short-long contrast, no neat-bow close
dimension_notes.framework: pass — nut-graf structure, facts → analysis → opinion, claims traceable to brief sources
